'Ndrina Mandalari.description: The 'Ndrina Mandalari is a 'ndrina (family) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Guardavalle, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in construction, real estate, international drug trafficking, money laundering, waste management, and public contracts. The group has strong ties to Freemasonry and operates in Italy as well as in New York, Nevada, and Arizona in the United States.

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Gallico.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei ... Gallico

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Molè.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei ... Molè

descriptions: The Mandalari are suspected to have contacts with the Morabito.
edges.evidence_quote: si suppone abbiano contatti anche con la 'Ndrina Morabito

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Palamara.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei ... Palamara

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Papalia.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei ... Papalia

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Romeo.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei ... Romeo

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Macrì.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei ... Macrì

descriptions: The Mandalari are allies of the Novella.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei Novella

descriptions: Zemunska/Bojović OCG is a regional clan within Škaljari OCG.
edges.evidence_quote: it operates as an association of highly independent regional clans - "Barska," OCG, "Budvanska" OCG, "Zemunska / Bojović" OCG.

HooNaRa.description: Far-right hooligan group based around German football club Chemnitzer FC, founded in the early 1990s by Thomas Haller. The group engaged in fights with other hooligan firms and was banned from the stadium in 2006. Officially dissolved in 2007, it continues as a loose network of 20-30 members.

descriptions: HooNaRa was founded by the same person who ran CFC Security, indicating a close association.
edges.evidence_quote: HooNaRa was founded in the early 1990s by Thomas Haller, who also ran the security service CFC Security.

descriptions: Michaels family emerged from remnants of the Cuckoo Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Two rival Lebanese crime families, the Michaels family and the Leisure family, emerged in St. Louis, Missouri from the remnants of the Cuckoo Gang, a bootlegging and gambling outfit which operated during the Prohibition era.

descriptions: Michaels family was affiliated with and under control of the Italian-American St. Louis crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Syrian-Lebanese crime families in St. Louis were affiliated with and under the control of the Italian-American St. Louis crime family headed by Anthony Giordano.

descriptions: Michaels and Leisure families engaged in open warfare after Giordano's death.
edges.evidence_quote: After Giordano's death from cancer on August 29, 1980, the Michaels and Leisure families entered into open warfare with one another.

descriptions: Ruga, Leuzzi, and Vallelonga formed a mafia consortium.
edges.evidence_quote: Il processo faida dei boschi sancisce la presenza di una consorteria mafiosa dei Ruga-Leuzzi-Vallelonga

Boodle Gang.description: American street gang active in New York City during the mid-to-late 19th century. Notorious as 'butcher cart thieves' in the 1850s, they hijacked food provision wagons and later robbed messengers in the financial district. The gang declined during gang wars in the 1890s and was defunct by the turn of the century.

descriptions: The Boodle Gang fared poorly in gang wars against the Hudson Dusters, who dominated the area by the end of the 1890s.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Potashes , Hudson Dusters

descriptions: Hudson Dusters displaced Boodle Gang from dominance in Lower West Side Manhattan.
edges.evidence_quote: They and the much older Boodle Gang dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s when they were displaced by the then emerging Hudson Dusters

descriptions: The Boodle Gang and Potashes were rival butcher cart mobs competing in the same area.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Potashes , Hudson Dusters

descriptions: Hudson Dusters fought against the Boodle Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the two gangs later became allies during the gang wars against Gay Nineties gangs, the Potashes and Boodle Gangs

descriptions: Infobox lists Boodle Gang as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Boodle Gang

descriptions: Potashes and Boodle Gang jointly dominated Lower West Side Manhattan.
edges.evidence_quote: They and the much older Boodle Gang dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s

descriptions: Aquino-Coluccio-Scali and Mazzaferro-Femia were in conflict in the Marina di Gioiosa Jonica feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Marina di Gioiosa Jonica, fra gli Aquino-Coluccio-Scali e i Mazzaferro-Femia negli anni '70.

descriptions: The Carelli are allied with the Zingari di Lauropoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati con gli zingari di Lauropoli e con i Critelli di Cariati.

descriptions: Forastefano was in a feud against the Zingari di Lauropoli.
edges.evidence_quote: È stata coinvolta alla fine degli anni novanta e all'inizio del nuovo secolo nella faida contro gli Zingari di Lauropoli

Slaughter House Gang.description: A prominent street gang in New York's Fourth Ward during the late 1880s to the mid-1990s, known for violent muggings and murder. They lured victims into dive bars or used a bucket of ash to blind them before robbing and sometimes killing them. The gang was led by George Leese, a river pirate and illegal prize fight organizer. They dominated the Fourth Ward until police drove them out after the American Civil War.

descriptions: Gallico is allied with Sgrò.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono in alleanza con i Morgante, gli Sgrò e gli Sciglitano

descriptions: Raiders MC is an official support club under the Mongols.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mongols oversee the Raiders MC, an affiliated official support club.

descriptions: The Cutters clashed with the Red Army in 2005, known as 'The Battle of Everton Valley'.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cutters have been involved in a number of high-profile clashes with other firms, most notably in 2005 with the Red Army, a firm associated with Manchester United.

descriptions: Red Army fought with the Inter City Firm.
edges.evidence_quote: It shows the Red Army fighting with the Inter City Firm (ICF) around Manchester.

descriptions: Young Munichs is a sub-division of the Red Army.
edges.evidence_quote: there are also sub-divisions of the firm known as the Young Munichs, the Inter City Jibbers (ICJ), the M58 Firm and the Moston Rats.

descriptions: Inter City Jibbers is a sub-division of the Red Army.
edges.evidence_quote: there are also sub-divisions of the firm known as the Young Munichs, the Inter City Jibbers (ICJ), the M58 Firm and the Moston Rats.

descriptions: M58 Firm is a sub-division of the Red Army.
edges.evidence_quote: there are also sub-divisions of the firm known as the Young Munichs, the Inter City Jibbers (ICJ), the M58 Firm and the Moston Rats.

descriptions: Moston Rats is a sub-division of the Red Army.
edges.evidence_quote: there are also sub-divisions of the firm known as the Young Munichs, the Inter City Jibbers (ICJ), the M58 Firm and the Moston Rats.

Società di Polistena.description: The Società di Polistena is a 'ndrangheta criminal organization based in Polistena, Calabria, Italy, with influence in surrounding towns. It has been active since the late 19th century, with early recorded bosses including Pasquale Ferrara and Francesco Sergio. The group engaged in drug and arms trafficking, and was involved in reconstruction profiteering after the 1905 earthquake. It remains active, with historical ties to the Guerrisi and Mercuri families.

descriptions: Società di Polistena is a criminal organization of 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La società di Polistena (o Locale di Polistena) è un'organizzazione criminale di 'ndrangheta

descriptions: Società di Polistena is dependent on the Mandamento tirrenico.
edges.evidence_quote: dipendente dal Mandamento tirrenico

descriptions: Riccardo Sibio of the Fasciani clan often worked for Banda della Magliana members.
edges.evidence_quote: Riccardo Sibio, appartenente al clan dei Fasciani e spesso al servizio anche dei componenti della Banda

descriptions: Mezzatesta and Scalise were in conflict in the Reventino feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida del Reventino , tra le cosche Mezzatesta e Scalise.

descriptions: Infobox lists Eleventh Hour RC as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Eleventh Hour RC

descriptions: Hells Angels forced the Apollos biker gang to disband in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2000, the Hells Angels forced the disbanding of the Apollos biker gang in Regina.

Morello crime family.description: One of the earliest crime families in New York City, based in Italian Harlem. Founded by Giuseppe Morello in the 1890s, it dominated the Italian underworld by defeating the Brooklyn Camorra. The family engaged in racketeering, counterfeiting, and murder, and was the predecessor of the Genovese crime family. It was active until 1931.

descriptions: The Morello crime family founded the 116th Street Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Founders: Morello crime family

descriptions: Camorra rivaled the Morello crime family in New York City.
edges.evidence_quote: They rivaled the now defunct Morello crime family for power in New York City.

descriptions: The Coney Island gang and Navy Street gang were in conflict with the Morello family.
edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets

descriptions: The infobox lists the Morello crime family as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Morello crime family

descriptions: D'Aquila was originally a crew of the Morello family before splitting off.
edges.evidence_quote: originally a crew of the Morello family prior to breaking off

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Morello crime family

descriptions: Lucchese family originated from the Morello crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The early history of the Lucchese crime family can be traced back to the Morello crime family

descriptions: The Morello family fought the Camorra in the Mafia–Camorra War.
edges.evidence_quote: Gaetano "Tommaso/Thomas" Lo Monte took control of the policy game in Harlem but murdered on October 13, 1915. Nicholo Morello took over and became embroiled in the Mafia-Camorra War . This conflict was between the Sicilian Morello-Terranova family and Brooklyn Camorra gangs led by Pellegrino Morano .

descriptions: The Morello family defeated the Camorra in a conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morellos were based in Manhattan 's Italian Harlem and eventually gained dominance in the Italian underworld by defeating the rival Neapolitan Camorra of Brooklyn .

descriptions: The D'Aquila crime family is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Brooklyn Camorra , D'Aquila crime family , Schiro crime family

descriptions: The Morello family was the predecessor of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: They were the predecessors of what eventually became known as the Genovese crime family .

descriptions: Morello family member Eugene Ubriaco and Nicholas Morello met with the Navy Street gang and were killed.
edges.evidence_quote: On September 7, 1916, Ubriaco along with Nicholas Morello meet with the Navy Street gang in Brooklyn and they both were shot to death on Johnson Street in Brooklyn.

descriptions: The Schiro crime family is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Brooklyn Camorra , D'Aquila crime family , Schiro crime family

descriptions: The Navy Street gang and Coney Island gang were in conflict with the Morello family.
edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets

descriptions: The White Hand Gang formed to counter the Morello crime family's encroachment on the waterfront.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1900s, with Italian criminal organizations such as the Morello crime family encroaching on the waterfront, various Irish gangs united to form the White Hand Gang.

Crazy Butch Gang.description: American juvenile street gang active in New York City's Lower East Side during the late 19th century, known for pickpocketing and sneak thievery. Founded by Simon Erenstoft ("Crazy Butch") around 1890, the gang was allied with the Eastman Gang and broke up after Butch's death in a gunfight.

descriptions: The Crazy Butch Gang was allied with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s as they were incorporated into the Squab Wheelmen , an ally of Monk Eastman's organization.

descriptions: The Crazy Butch Gang was incorporated into the Squab Wheelmen.
edges.evidence_quote: as they were incorporated into the Squab Wheelmen , an ally of Monk Eastman's organization.

Alkhalil family.description: The Alkhalil family is a Palestinian Canadian organized crime family based in Surrey, British Columbia. Originating from Palestinian refugees who fled to Lebanon and later Saudi Arabia, they immigrated to Canada in 1990. The family has been involved in gang violence, including a long-standing feud with the Duhre brothers. Several family members have been murdered or convicted of crimes.

descriptions: Alkhalil family members were tried for murdering members of the rival Dhak-Duhre gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Starting on 29 June 2021, Amero and Alkhalil were on trial in Vancouver for the 2012 murders of the rival gangsters Sandip Duhre and Suhhveer Dhak

descriptions: The Alkhalil family feud with the Dhak-Duhre group started after Khalil's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Naud was working as a drug dealer for the Dhak-Duhre group, and the killing of Khalil started a feud.

descriptions: The Alkhalil family and Duhre brothers have a long-standing feud.
time_periods: since the beginning of the century
edges.evidence_quote: A long-standing feud with the three Duhre brothers started at the beginning of the century.

descriptions: Mahmoud Alkhalil was killed in a shoot-out with the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Mahmoud Alkhalil was a member of the Independent Soldiers street gang. On 16 August 2003, he was shot during a shoot-out with the Hells Angels on the dance floor of the Loft Six nightclub in Gastown.

descriptions: Nabil Alkhalil acted as liaison between the Alkhalil family and the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Nabil Alkhalil is believed to have served as the Alkhalil family's liaison with the Sinaloa Cartel.

descriptions: Alkhalil family was a member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Other members of the Wolfpack Alliance were the Alkhalil family of Montreal and Ottawa

descriptions: Alkhalil family members joined the Wolfpack, with Nabil serving as liaison to Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack soon took in other members, most notably the Alkhalil family

descriptions: The Alkhalil family is part of the Wolfpack Alliance leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Nations gang came to be involved in a violent feud with the Wolfpack Alliance led by Larry Amero and Rabih "Robby" Alkhalil of the Alkhalil family.

descriptions: Somali Outlaws and Madhiban With Attitude are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hot Boyz , Madhiban With Attitude , 1627 Boys

descriptions: Al-Bara Battalion is listed as an opponent of RSF.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Al-Bara Battalion

descriptions: Kudo-kai annexed Kusano-ikka after prior conflicts.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1987, the Kudo-kai annexed the Kusano-ikka, a Kitakyushu-based yakuza clan which had frequent violent conflicts with the Kudo-kai until then.

descriptions: The text lists Street Soldiers as an enemy of Wheels of Soul.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemies of the Wheels of Soul include the Outcast Motorcycle Club, Sin City Titans, Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club and the Street Soldiers.

descriptions: The Junior Black Mafia is a successor or later iteration of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Years active: c. 1969–1984 , c. 1985–1992 (JBM)

descriptions: Black Mafia members created the JBM as a successor organization.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1985, members of the original Black Mafia organized African-American youths into the Junior Black Mafia (JBM), also known as the Young Black Mafia, in order to counter and regain territory from New York-based Jamaican posses who had rapidly taken control of drug distribution in the black neighborhoods of Philadelphia.

descriptions: JBM cooperates with Bruno-Scarfo crime family in cocaine distribution.
edges.evidence_quote: The JBM cooperates with associates of the Italian-American Bruno-Scarfo Mafia crime family in the distribution of cocaine and appears to have modeled its criminal methods after that LCN organization as well as the original Black Mafia, relying heavily on violence and extortion to further its drug enterprise.

descriptions: JBM fought a drug war against the Shower Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: The JBM was involved in a drug war with the Shower Posse in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Junior Black Mafia

descriptions: Merlino aligned with Junior Black Mafia members.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1990s, Merlino was also aligned with members of the Junior Black Mafia.

descriptions: The Shower Posse was involved in a drug war with the Junior Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shower Posse was involved in a drug war with the Junior Black Mafia in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.

Tren del Llano.description: Venezuelan criminal organization founded by José Antonio Tovar Colina alias 'El Picure' in 2008, initially operating in Guárico and Aragua as a car theft gang. Known as one of the first megagangs, it engages in drug trafficking, extortion, homicide, and kidnapping. The group opposes Nicolás Maduro's rule and has threatened opposition leader María Corina Machado. It remains active with an estimated 700 members.

descriptions: El infobox lista al Tren del Llano como aliado del Tren de Aragua.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Tren del Llano

descriptions: Tren del Llano displaced Los Caraqueños, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Para mediados del 2020, el grupo expandió sus actividades al estado de Sucre, desplazando a una célula criminal llamada "Los Caraqueños" ligada al Tren de Aragua.

descriptions: Tren del Llano is allied with Tren de Aragua.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tren de Aragua

descriptions: The infobox lists Tren de Aragua as an ally of Tren del Llano.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: El Tren de Aragua

Iron Horsemen Motorcycle Club.description: American outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1965 in Cincinnati, Ohio. Known for drug trafficking and violent crimes, considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang. Members have been convicted for drug dealing and murder, with operations spanning multiple states.

descriptions: Bandidos and Iron Horsemen engaged in a shootout over a drug dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos and members of the Iron Horsemen exchanged gunfire due to a drug dispute at a motorcycle rally near Zillah on June 18, 2000.

descriptions: Iron Horsemen is a smaller motorcycle gang allied with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels are allied with numerous smaller motorcycle gangs – such as the ... Iron Horsemen

descriptions: Infobox lists Iron Horsemen MC as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Devils Diciples MC, Diablos MC, El Forastero MC, Galloping Goose MC, Hells Angels MC, Highwaymen MC, Iron Horsemen MC, Loners MC, Mongols MC, Pagans MC, Red Devils MC, Sons of Silence MC, Warlocks MC

descriptions: The Iron Horsemen are a rival biker gang of the Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival biker gangs of the Outlaws include the Devils Diciples, the Diablos, El Forastero, the Galloping Goose, the Hells Angels, the Highwaymen, the Iron Horsemen

descriptions: Outlaws had a violent rivalry with Iron Horsemen, including bombings.
edges.evidence_quote: A violent rivalry between the Outlaws and the Iron Horsemen in Greater Cincinnati began in the late 1960s.

descriptions: Outlaws and Iron Horsemen have a history of conflict, including assaults and a shootout.
edges.evidence_quote: The conflict between the Outlaws and the Hells Angels resulted in at least twelve homicides in Northeast Ohio in the years leading up to 1986.

descriptions: Sons of Silence is allied with Iron Horsemen.
edges.evidence_quote: The club is allied with the Hells Angels and the Iron Horsemen

descriptions: Members of both clubs traveled together, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: While travelling to an annual motorcycle rally in Sturgis, South Dakota on August 5, 1997, seven Sons of Silence and Iron Horsemen members were stopped by South Dakota Highway Patrol troopers...
